rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE CHUNG HOP WONG AND KING TSUI LAU WONG REVOCABL TRUST dated sep 21, 2009 amended Sep 26 2009
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To The Petition Of Helen Wong-Joe, Jessica Wong, And Brandon Wong, Petitioners, For Compensatory Damages Against Kathryn Wong For Elder Abuse; Undue Influence; Conversion; Misrepresentation; Misappropriation; Breach Of Fiduciary Duties; To Compel Respondent To Provide Information Concerning Trust (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: [part 2 of 4- Wong Trust] Respondent's demurrer for Elder Abuse/Undue Influence, on the ground of failure to state a cause of action, is OVERRULED, insofar as Petitioners intended to bring a cause of action for financial Elder Abuse as a result of undue influence with respect to the transfer of the 6th Avenue property. The Petition alleges Respondent held a confidential or fiduciary position relative to Settlor Chung at the time the Grant Deed was drafted and executed, due to Decedent at the relevant times being in the vulnerable position of advanced age, newly-widowed, non-English speaking, and reliant on Respondent's aid with the family business. Petitioners allege that at the time Settlor Chung signed the grant deed transferring the property to Respondent he was in state of cognitive decline. With respect to the Balboa Street property, Petitioners failed to allege facts showing that the Settlors were mentally vulnerable at the time of the transfer, and failed to allege facts showing Respondent exerted any undue influence on the Settlors in any way at the time of the transfer. However, a demurrer must dispose of an entire cause of action to be sustained. (PH II, Inc. v. Superior Court (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1682.) Since the allegations are sufficient to allege a cause of action for Elder Abuse as a result of undue influence with respect to the transfer of the 6th Avenue property, this is all that is needed to survive demurrer. Notwithstanding, in the interest of efficiency, Petitioners are granted leave to add allegations to supporting a claim for Elder Abuse/Undue Influence as to the transfer of the Balboa Street property. Respondent's demurrer on the ground of statute of limitations is OVERRULED. Respondent contends the Elder Abuse and Conversion claims as to the transfer of the Balboa Street property is barred by the four-year and three-year statute of limitations, respectively. On its face, the Petition does not allege any facts showing that the claim as to the transfer of Balboa Street is necessarily barred by the statute of limitations. Moreover, a demurrer must dispose of an entire cause of action to be sustained. (PH II, Inc. v. Superior Court (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1682.) There are several claims contained in the first cause of action, including the allegedly wrongful transfer of the 6th Avenue property, which Respondent does not argue is barred by the statute of limitations. Notwithstanding, Respondent's argument is well-taken. Petitioners are granted leave to plead the date of discovery of the allegedly wrongful transfers. Respondent's demurrer on the ground of laches is OVERRULED. It does not affirmatively appear from the face of the Petition that the doctrine applies as a matter of law. (Zakaessian v. Zakaessian (1945) 70 Cal.App.2d 721, 726.) Second Cause of Action: Misrepresentation and Fraud: Respondent's special demurrer on the ground of uncertainty is SUSTAINED with leave to amend, insofar as Petitioners intended to bring a cause of action for constructive fraud as opposed to the wholly distinct claim of actual fraud. The elements of constructive fraud are not pleaded on the face of the Petition. Any and all allegations made "on information and belief" must be supported by information as to what leads Petitioners to believe that the allegations are true. (see part 3 of 4)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE CHUNG HOP WONG AND KING TSUI LAU WONG REVOCABL TRUST dated sep 21, 2009 amended Sep 26 2009
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To The Petition Of Helen Wong-Joe, Jessica Wong, And Brandon Wong, Petitioners, For Compensatory Damages Against Kathryn Wong For Elder Abuse; Undue Influence; Conversion; Misrepresentation; Misappropriation; Breach Of Fiduciary Duties; To Compel Respondent To Provide Information Concerning Trust
rulings.Rulings: [part 1 of 4- Wong Trust] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the Court is Respondent Kathryn Wong's Special and General Demurrer to Helen Wong-Joe, Jessica Wong and Brandon Wong's Petition for Compensatory Damages Against Kathryn Wong for Elder Abuse; Undue Influence; Conversion; Misrepresentation; Misappropriation; Breach of Fiduciary Duties; To Compel Respondent to Provide Information Concerning the Trust. Respondent demurs specially to the Petition on the ground of uncertainty under Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(f), specifically on grounds that the Petition is so unclear, uncertain, ambiguous, and unintelligible that Respondent cannot reasonably respond. Respondent demurs generally to all causes of action Respondent infers from the Petition, on grounds of failure to allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action, lack of standing, statute of limitations, and laches. As described in more detail below, the demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to some claims, and OVERRULED as to others. The Court notes a complete breakdown in the meet and confer process preceding this demurrer. Respondent sent a letter to Petitioners outlining the defects in the Petition. Petitioners did not concede any defects, and made no amendment. Then, after the demurrer was filed, Petitioners filed an opposition that was replete with concessions, implicit and explicit, that there were defects in the Petition, and suggestions that they should receive an opportunity to amend to correct them. Judicial resources would have been conserved had Petitioners responded to the meet and confer letter in a more forthright manner. In considering a demurrer, the court takes as true all the ultimate facts properly noticed and alleged in the pleading, regardless of how improbable or unlikely such facts might seem. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318; Serrano v. Priest (1971) 5 Cal.3d 584, 591; see also Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604). "[I]f, on consideration of all facts stated, it appears that plaintiff is entitled to any relief against defendant, complaint will be held good, though facts may not be clearly stated, or may be intermingled with statement of other facts irrelevant to cause of action shown, or though plaintiff may demand relief to which he is not entitled under facts alleged." (Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236, quoting Matteson v. Wagoner (1905) 147 Cal. 739, 742.) First Cause of Action: Elder Abuse, Undue Influence, and Conversion: Respondent's special demurrer on the ground of uncertainty is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. It is wholly unclear whether Petitioners are alleging a claim for financial Elder Abuse by way of conversion or whether they are pleading a separate cause of action for Conversion. The confusion is compounded by the fact that Petitioners allege a separate cause of action for Misappropriation. Further, uncertainty lies in connection with Petitioner's failure to allege matters essential to a cause of action for Conversion. Petitioners fail to plead facts showing (i) Respondent assumed control or ownership over the jewelry, and (ii) that Petitioners and/or the (revocable) Trust had an immediate right to possession of the property. (See, Petition, 31, 36, 86, 113.) Any and all allegations made "on information and belief" must be supported by information as to what leads Petitioners to believe that the allegations are true. (see part 2 of 4)

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To The Petition Of Helen Wong-Joe, Jessica Wong, And Brandon Wong, Petitioners, For Compensatory Damages Against Kathryn Wong For Elder Abuse; Undue Influence; Conversion; Misrepresentation; Misappropriation; Breach Of Fiduciary Duties; To Compel Respondent To Provide Information Concerning Trust (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: [part 4 of 4- Wong Trust] Petitioners shall file an amended Petition within 15 days of the date of entry of this order. Respondent shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. (end of tentative ruling)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE CHUNG HOP WONG AND KING TSUI LAU WONG REVOCABL TRUST dated sep 21, 2009 amended Sep 26 2009
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To The Petition Of Helen Wong-Joe, Jessica Wong, And Brandon Wong, Petitioners, For Compensatory Damages Against Kathryn Wong For Elder Abuse; Undue Influence; Conversion; Misrepresentation; Misappropriation; Breach Of Fiduciary Duties; To Compel Respondent To Provide Information Concerning Trust (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: [part 3 of 4- Wong Trust] Respondent's demurrer on the ground of lack of standing is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The Court cannot discern from the Petition whether the fraud claim is tied to the elder abuse claim under the Welfare and Institutions Code as set forth in the First Cause of Action; or if Petitioners are alleging a separate cause of action for fraud, for which they have not established standing based on their cited authority, Estate of Lowrie (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 220, which conferred standing to bring an elder abuse action against a trustee under the Elder Abuse Act, not necessarily a separate cause of action for fraud, or constructive fraud. Third Cause of Action: Misappropriation of Funds: Respondent's special demurrer on the ground of uncertainty is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Misappropriation is not a cause of action but rather a variety of Conversion. The allegations supporting the cause of action appear to be the same allegations supporting Petitioner's claim for Conversion. To the extent the claim for misappropriation is duplicative of the conversion claim, Petitioners are granted leave to amend to reorganize the Petition. Any and all allegations made "on information and belief" must be supported by information as to what leads Petitioners to believe that the allegations are true. Respondent's demurrer on the ground of lack of standing is OVERRULED for the same reasons stated under the First Cause of Action. Respondent's demurrer on the ground of laches is OVERRULED for the same reasons stated for under First Cause of Action. Fourth Cause of Action: Self-Dealing and Breach of Fiduciary Duties: Respondent's special demurrer on the ground of uncertainty is OVERRULED. It can be reasonably inferred from the face of the Petition that Petitioners are alleging Breach of Fiduciary Duties by Self-Dealing based on (i) Respondent's position as agent and Trustee de son tort and (ii) the allegations of undue influence by Respondent over Settlors in securing the transfer of trust properties. Respondent's demurrer on the ground of failure to state a cause of action is OVERRULED. Fifth Cause of Action: Order Requiring Respondent to Provide Requested Information: Respondent's demurrer on the ground that the claim is premature is OVERRULED. (Probate Code Sec 17200(a)(7)(B).) Respondent's contention that the issue is premature is not supported by any authority. Sixth Cause of Action: Imposition of Constructive Trust, Unjust Enrichment, and Interference: Respondent's demurrer on the ground of failure to state a cause of action is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Constructive trust is not a cause of action, but rather a judicial remedy. California does not recognize a cause of action for unjust enrichment. (Hooked Media Group, Inc. v. Apple Inc. (2020) 55 Cal.App.5th 323; Melchior v. New Line Productions, Inc. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 779, 793 [the phrase "unjust enrichment" does not describe a theory of recovery; it is a general principle underlying various legal doctrines and remedies].) Finally, it is not discernible from the face of the Petition what claim or cause of action is being alleged by "Interference." (see part 4 of 4)
